title: scutellaria ovalifolia subsp. hirsuta
intro: Scutellaria ovalifolia subsp. hirsuta, also known as the hairy skullcap, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to China. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The hairy skullcap is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
